inputSchema.description: List all chat channels the user has access to. Returns channel names, IDs, and types. Use this tool to discover available channels for reading messages or participating in conversations.
inputSchema.title: ListChannels

channels.description: The list of channels the user has access to

items.description: A channel item returned by the list channels tool

channelType.description: The type of channel (public, private, organization, or direct_message)

name.description: The channel's display name (may be None for direct messages)

inputSchema.description: Search for items by their content. For documents, this searches the document body text. For emails, this searches the email message body. For chats, this searches the message content. This tool finds items based on what's inside them, not their titles or names.

entityTypes.description: Which types of items to search. Leave empty to search all types. Examples: ['documents'], ['emails', 'documents'], ['channels']

query.description: The text content to search for. This searches within the body of documents, emails, and messages.

inputSchema.title: ContentSearch

oneOf.description:
DocumentSearchResponseItem object with document metadata we fetch from macrodb. we don't store these
timestamps in opensearch as they would require us to update document page record
every time the document updates (specifically for updated_at and viewed_at)

document_search_results.description:
The search results for the document
This may be empty if the search result match was on the document name only

items.description: A document match for a given node

bcc.description: The highlight match on the bcc (email only)

cc.description: The highlight match on the cc (email only)

content.description: The highlight match on the content field

name.description: The highlight match on the name field

recipients.description: The highlight match on the recipients (email only)

sender.description: The highlight match on the sender (email only)

user_id.description: The highlight match on the user (owner) of the entity

node_id.description:
The node id for the document.
This is only useful for markdown at the moment
This will only be provided if the match was on content

raw_content.description:
The raw content of the document.
This is only included for markdown files and will be the raw json node of the match

file_type.description: The file type of the document

id.description:
Standardized fields that all item types will share.
These field names are being aligned across all item types
for consistency in our data model.

metadata.description: Metadata from the database. None if the document doesn't exist in the database.

anyOf.description:
The document sub type enum represents all values of document sub types.
These values should match the `document_sub_type_value` table in macrodb.

oneOf.description: A task document

sub_type.description: The sub type of the document if present.

oneOf.description:
ChatSearchResponse object with channel metadata we fetch from macrodb. we don't store these
timestamps in opensearch as they would require us to update each chat message record for the chat
every time the chat updates (specifically for updated_at and viewed_at)

chat_search_results.description:
The search results for the chat
This may be empty if the search result match was on the chat title only

items.description: A chat match for a given message id

chat_message_id.description:
The chat message id for the chat
This is only present if the search match was on the chat message content

highlight.description: The highlights for the chat message

bcc.description: The highlight match on the bcc (email only)

cc.description: The highlight match on the cc (email only)

content.description: The highlight match on the content field

name.description: The highlight match on the name field

recipients.description: The highlight match on the recipients (email only)

sender.description: The highlight match on the sender (email only)

user_id.description: The highlight match on the user (owner) of the entity

role.description:
The role of the chat message
This is only present if the search match was on the chat message content

id.description:
Standardized fields that all item types will share.
These field names are being aligned across all item types
for consistency in our data model.

metadata.description: Metadata from the database. None if the chat doesn't exist in the database.

user_id.description: The id of the creator of the chat

oneOf.description:
EmailSearchResponseItem object with email metadata we fetch from email service. we don't store these
timestamps in opensearch as they would require us to update each email message record for the thread
every time the thread updates (specifically for updated_at and viewed_at)

email_message_search_results.description:
The search results for the document
This may be empty if the search result match was on the email subject only

items.description: A email message match for a given thread id

bcc.description: This is only present if the search result is on the message content

cc.description: This is only present if the search result is on the message content

highlight.description: The highlights for the email message

bcc.description: The highlight match on the bcc (email only)

cc.description: The highlight match on the cc (email only)

content.description: The highlight match on the content field

name.description: The highlight match on the name field

recipients.description: The highlight match on the recipients (email only)

sender.description: The highlight match on the sender (email only)

user_id.description: The highlight match on the user (owner) of the entity

labels.description: This is only present if the search result is on the message content

message_id.description:
The email message id.
This is only present if the search result is on the message content

pretty_sender.description:
The pretty sender.
If the match is on the subject, the pretty sender is the latest sender on the thread.
This could be the sender's email if there is no contact name for the sender.

recipients.description: This is only present if the search result is on the message content

sender.description:
The sender.
If the match is on the subject, the sender is the latest sender on the thread.

sent_at.description:
When the email message was sent
This is only present if the search result is on the message content

id.description:
Standardized fields that all item types will share.
These field names are being aligned across all item types
for consistency in our data model.

subject.description:
The subject of the email
This is only present if the search result is on the message content

thread_id.description: The id of the email thread

user_id.description: The id of the owner of the email thread

oneOf.description:
ChannelSearchResponseItem object with channel metadata we fetch from macrodb. we don't store these
timestamps in opensearch as they would require us to update each chat message record for the chat
every time the chat updates (specifically for updated_at and viewed_at and interacted_at)

channel_message_search_results.description:
The search results for the channel
This may be empty if the search result match was not on content

items.description: A channel message match for a given channel id

created_at.description:
When the channel message was created
This is only prsent if the search result is on the message content

highlight.description: The highlights for the channel message

bcc.description: The highlight match on the bcc (email only)

cc.description: The highlight match on the cc (email only)

content.description: The highlight match on the content field

name.description: The highlight match on the name field

recipients.description: The highlight match on the recipients (email only)

sender.description: The highlight match on the sender (email only)

user_id.description: The highlight match on the user (owner) of the entity

message_id.description:
The channel message id
This is only prsent if the search result is on the message content

sender_id.description:
The sender id
This is only prsent if the search result is on the message content

updated_at.description:
When the channel message was last updated
This is only prsent if the search result is on the message content

id.description:
Standardized fields that all item types will share.
These field names are being aligned across all item types
for consistency in our data model.

metadata.description: Metadata from the database. None if the channel doesn't exist in the database.

owner_id.description: we don't store this for channels atm but keeping it here for consistency

oneOf.description:
ProjectSearchResponseItem object with project metadata we fetch from macrodb. we don't store these
timestamps in opensearch as they would require us to update the project record
every time the project updates (specifically for updated_at and viewed_at)

id.description:
Standardized fields that all item types will share.
These field names are being aligned across all item types
for consistency in our data model.

metadata.description: Metadata from the database. None if the project doesn't exist in the database.

bcc.description: The highlight match on the bcc (email only)

cc.description: The highlight match on the cc (email only)

content.description: The highlight match on the content field

name.description: The highlight match on the name field

sender.description: The highlight match on the sender (email only)

user_id.description: The highlight match on the user (owner) of the entity

inputSchema.description: List the emails the user has access to. Use this tool to find all emails from / to a specific person. Prefer using the search tool to search on a specific matching string within the content or the name of the entity.

cursor.description:
A preview response will tell you what the next cursor id is.
        If expected emails are not in the current response and it gives you a cursor id, use this field to list the next page of emails. If no cursor id is provided you've reached the end

limit.description: limit the max emails returned. This defaults to 20 and has a maximum of 500

sortMethod.description: Sort response by one of: viewed_at | updated_at | created_at | viewed_updated. Prefer viewed_at unless the otherwise specified

view.description: Choose the view to list emails from. One of either standard label or user label must be specified, otherwise defaults to important.

standardLabel.description: Prefer important or all. Leave empty if using a user label

userLabel.description: Choose a custom user label to filter by (optional). Standard label will take precedence over user label if both are specified.

inputSchema.title: ListEmails

calendar_invite.description: if any email contains a calendar invite

generic_sender.description: if any sender in the thread is a generic email

known_sender.description: if user has previously emailed any sender in the thread

tabular.description: if any email contains a <table> html tag

inputSchema.description:
Read content by ID(s). Supports reading documents, channels, chats, and emails by their respective IDs. Use this tool when you need to retrieve the full content of a specific item(s).
    Channel transcripts only include 300 messages. Use 'messages_since' to see messages in a different time window.

contentType.description: The type of content to read. Choose based on the type of content you want to retrieve.

ids.description: ID(s) of the content to read. IMPORTANT: document, channel-message, chat-message, and email-message content types support MULTIPLE ids! For all other content types (channel, chat-thread, email-thread) provide a single id.

messagesSince.description: A local datetime of the earliest message to include in a channel transcript ex: 2025-11-25 12:00:09 EST, only applicable to channels

inputSchema.title: Read

inputSchema.description: Search for items by their name or title. For documents, this searches the document name. For emails, this searches the subject line. For chats, this searches the chat title. For projects (folders), this searches the project name. This tool finds items based on what they're called, not their content.

entityTypes.description: Which types of items to search. Leave empty to search all types. Examples: ['documents'], ['emails', 'documents'], ['channels']

name.description: The name or title to search for. For emails, this is the subject line. For channels, this can be the channel name or participant names.

inputSchema.title: NameSearch

inputSchema.description: List documents the user has access to with optional filtering and pagination. Only applies to documents, not emails, AI conversations, chat/slack threads, projects aka folders. This tool returns document metadata including access levels and supports filtering by file type, minimum access level, and pagination. Use this tool to discover and browse documents before using the Read tool to access their content. Prefer using the search tool to search on a specific matching string within the content or the name of the entity.

exhaustiveSearch.description: Exhaustive search to get all results. Defaults to false. Set to true when you need all matching documents, ignoring pagination limits.

fileTypes.description: Document file types to include. Examples: ['pdf'], ['md', 'txt']. Leave empty to include all document types.

minAccessLevel.description: Minimum access level required. Defaults to 'view' if not specified.

pageOffset.description: Page offset for pagination. Default is 0. Use higher values to get subsequent pages of results.

pageSize.description: Number of results per page. Max is 100, default is 50. Use smaller values for focused results.

inputSchema.title: ListDocuments

results.description: The list results (truncated to `results_returned` limit if applicable)

access_level.description: The user's access level to this document

deleted_at.description: When the document was deleted (null if not deleted)

file_type.description: The file type of the document

project_id.description: The project id containing the document

updated_at.description: When the document was last updated

inputSchema.description:
Instruct an agent to edit a markdown file identified by an id.
    This tool should be used when the user include a markdown file in context and requests a revision or edit to that file

instructions.description: Instructions for the revision agent to follow to edit the markdown. These instructions will be provided by the user.

markdown_file_id.description: The markdown file id to target for editing. This file id will be in your context and labeled as a markdown (md) document

inputSchema.title: MarkdownRewrite

inputSchema.description: This is the expected shape of the streamed json following a `server_tool_use` in content_block_start event
inputSchema.title: WebSearchToolCall

SearchResult.description: A single search result from web search

outputSchema.description: Web search response content returned by Claude when using the web_search tool

content.description:
The search query that was executed
Array of search results

outputSchema.title: WebSearchResponse

inputSchema.description: The expected shape of the streamed JSON following a `server_tool_use` in content_block_start event
inputSchema.title: WebFetchToolCall

WebFetchContent.description: Content of a web fetch response - either a successful result or an error

WebFetchDocument.description: Document content from a web fetch

WebFetchErrorCode.description: Possible error codes for web fetch failures

oneOf.description: Invalid URL format
URL exceeds maximum length (250 characters)
URL blocked by domain filtering rules and model restrictions
Failed to fetch content (HTTP error)
Rate limit exceeded
Content type not supported (only text and PDF)
Maximum web fetch tool uses exceeded
An internal error occurred

WebFetchResult.description: Successful web fetch result containing the fetched content

retrieved_at.description: Timestamp when the content was retrieved

WebFetchSource.description: Source content of a fetched document

oneOf.description: Plain text content
Base64-encoded content (e.g., PDF)

WebFetchToolError.description: Error returned when web fetch fails

outputSchema.description: Web fetch tool response content returned by Claude when using the web_fetch tool
outputSchema.title: WebFetchResponse
